html:
Several Swedish royals are known by the name Sofia of Sweden:

  • Sofia Eriksdotter, princess, daughter of King Erik Knutsson, married Duchess of Mecklenburg.
  • Sofia Eriksdotter, Queen of Sweden, married to King Valdemar Birgersson, born into the Estrid family.
  • Sofia Vasa, princess, daughter of King Gustav I, married Princess of Saxe-Lauenburg.
  • Hedvig Sofia of Sweden, princess, daughter of Charles XI, married Regent of Holstein-Gottorp.
  • Sofia Albertina, princess, daughter of King Adolf Frederick, abbess.
  • Sofia Magdalena, Queen of Sweden, born into the House of Oldenburg.
  • Sofia of Sweden, princess, daughter of King Gustav IV Adolf, married Princess of Baden.
  • Sofia of Nassau, Queen of Sweden and Norway, born into the House of Orange-Nassau.
  • Sofia Hellqvist, married to Prince Carl Philip and therefore titled Princess Sofia.

== See also == Princess Sofia (various meanings)

How certain are we about this?

Information about people from the 19th century is very well documented (census records, newspapers, letters). The uncertainty mainly concerns selection and perspective – whose stories are highlighted.
tldr: Throughout history, many women have borne the name Sofia of Sweden. Among them are former queens, princesses, and the current Princess Sofia.
lattlast:
The text explains that several different individuals have held the title "Sofia of Sweden" throughout history. This can be confusing because the name has been used by women from various periods and with different roles within the royal family.

Among these women are both queens who ruled the country and princesses who married kings or princes. One of the people mentioned is the modern-day Princess Sofia, who is married to Prince Carl Philip.

Historians know a great deal about the individuals from the 19th century because many old documents and newspapers have survived. What is more difficult to determine with certainty is which stories are chosen to be told today.
